name: uses slack formatting
response: Here are the main features of this bot:  *Code & Repository* - Read, search, edit, and write files across codebases - Git operations — commits, branches, diffs, PRs - Azure DevOps integration — create/review PRs, manage work items, search commits - Automated PR reviews with structured feedback  *Project Management* - Jira integration — search/create/update issues, manage sprints, link work items - Quip document access and editing - Incident report generation from Jira tickets or Slack threads  *Observability & Debugging* - Sentry error tracking — list issues, view events, analyze with Seer - Grafana dashboards & alerts — query Prometheus, inspect panels - GCP logging & monitoring — query logs, check metrics - New Relic — app health, slow transactions, custom NRQL queries - MongoDB health checks — connections, CPU, disk, replication - Tempo distributed tracing — search and retrieve traces - Systematic debugging workflows  *Testing & Deployment* - Brunt integration test writing and execution (OMS, CMS, Cart, Catalog, etc.) - Sandbox deployment via monorepo submodules + FIK - TDD workflow support  *Communication* - Slack messaging via Composio integration - Figma design-to-code workflows  *Development Workflows* - Brainstorming and planning before implementation - Code review (requesting and receiving) - Impact analysis — "what breaks if I change X?" - Refactoring guidance — rename, extract, split, move - Codebase exploration and architecture understanding - Git worktree isolation for feature work - Ruff lint error resolution for Python projects  *Infrastructure & Auth* - Auto-reauthentication for Azure DevOps and GCP/Kubernetes on token expiry - Kubernetes cluster context management - Persistent memory system for cross-session context  _sent by shadow.ai_

quality_scores.feedback: Response is factually accurate and comprehensive but suffers from poor Slack formatting (HTML entities, missing bullets/structure) and excessive verbosity that obscures the content.
